inputRedacted.instruction:
Objective: Research developments in the Middle East war from the last 7 days (approx. 2026-03-08 through 2026-03-15) and write a neutral 800-word article summarizing the major military, political, humanitarian, and diplomatic dimensions. Include a distinct section on AI's role (examples: targeting, surveillance, information operations, defense systems). Requirements and constraints:

1) Timeframe: prioritise reporting and official releases dated within the last 7 days (2026-03-08 to 2026-03-15 inclusive). If a crucial background citation is older, label it as background.
2) Sources: prioritize reputable outlets and primary sources (Reuters, AP, BBC, Al Jazeera, AFP, NYT, Washington Post, ICRC, UN OCHA, official ministry/DoD statements, credible think tanks like IISS/CSIS). Include direct URLs for each citation. Aim to fetch up to 10-12 pages (maxPages <= 12).
3) Structure: short headline; ~800 words total (target exactly 800 words if possible; acceptable ±5%). Sections: Opening summary (2–3 paragraphs); Military developments (recent operations, territorial changes, casualty figures with dates/sources); Political developments (leadership/coalition moves, domestic politics, sanctions

Reason: The operation was aborted due to timeout.
Requested format: blog_post; tone: clear and practical; audience: general technical audience.

2. Add more accessible sources if paywalls/blocks are limiting content.

availableAgents.name: lead_agent
availableAgents.description: Specialist agent for lead generation and enrichment workflows.

availableAgents.name: research_agent
availableAgents.description: Specialist for web research, synthesis, and drafting.

availableTools.name: doc_qa
availableTools.description: Answer questions from local documentation/files with citations and bounded context.
availableTools.inputHint: Use for repo/docs questions before coding; pass scopePaths to narrow search.

exampleInput.question: What does the lead pipeline do?

availableTools.name: email_enrich
availableTools.description: Enrich current lead state with business emails from website/contact pages.
availableTools.inputHint: Use after lead discovery to improve email coverage; can run with or without browser fallback.

availableTools.name: file_edit
availableTools.description: Perform text replacement edits in an existing file.
availableTools.inputHint: Use for precise in-file edits when a direct find/replace is sufficient.

bounds: path must resolve inside project root

availableTools.name: file_list
availableTools.description: List files/directories inside the project root for navigation and planning edits.
availableTools.inputHint: Use before reading/editing files to discover relevant paths safely.

availableTools.name: file_read
availableTools.description: Read text file content from the project root with bounded slices.
availableTools.inputHint: Use for precise context gathering before making edits.

bounds: path must resolve inside project root

availableTools.name: file_write
availableTools.description: Write or append text content to a file inside the project root.
availableTools.inputHint: Use to create/update files after planning; keep writes scoped and reversible.

bounds: path must resolve inside project root

availableTools.name: lead_extract
availableTools.description: Extract structured leads from already-fetched pages stored in the current agent state.
availableTools.inputHint: Use after web_fetch when you want an atomic fetch -> extract flow instead of the full lead_pipeline.

availableTools.name: lead_pipeline
availableTools.description: Run the lead extraction pipeline over search + browse + extraction + quality gate.
availableTools.inputHint: Use when you need more validated leads. Increase maxPages for deeper crawl or tune minConfidence for stricter/looser gating.

availableTools.name: lead_search_shortlist
availableTools.description: Search and keep only fetch-worthy URLs before browsing, with objective-aware scoring (domain/content/geo/email signals) and host diversity.
availableTools.inputHint: Use before web_fetch when you want a lighter search -> shortlist -> fetch -> extract flow.

availableTools.name: process_list
availableTools.description: List local processes for diagnostics and lifecycle control.
availableTools.inputHint: Use with query filters before attempting process_stop.

availableTools.name: process_stop
availableTools.description: Stop a local process by PID (trusted mode only).
availableTools.inputHint: Use carefully after confirming target with process_list.

availableTools.name: recover_search
availableTools.description: Attempt recovery of the primary search provider (for example restarting local SearXNG).
availableTools.inputHint: Use when search provider appears down; then re-check status and retry search/lead_pipeline.

availableTools.name: run_diagnostics
availableTools.description: Analyze run timeline/tool telemetry and summarize likely failure points with recommendations.
availableTools.inputHint: Use after poor runs to pinpoint failure sources before retrying.

required: one of: runId | debugPath (optional if current run context is enough)

availableTools.name: search
availableTools.description: Run provider-backed web search and return top results.
availableTools.inputHint: Use to test/expand discovery queries before running deeper lead extraction.

availableTools.name: search_status
availableTools.description: Check live health of search providers and whether primary recovery is available.
availableTools.inputHint: Use when search-yield is low or search failures indicate provider outages/timeouts.

availableTools.name: shell_exec
availableTools.description: Execute a shell command in the project workspace (trusted mode only).
availableTools.inputHint: Use for diagnostics and local workflow commands. Avoid destructive operations.

availableTools.name: web_fetch
availableTools.description: Fetch and parse web pages using browser automation from query results or explicit URL list.
availableTools.inputHint: Use for deterministic page retrieval before extraction. Prefer maxPages <= 12 unless necessary.

payload.brief:
Objective: Research developments in the Middle East war from the last 7 days (approx. 2026-03-08 through 2026-03-15) and write a neutral 800-word article summarizing the major military, political, humanitarian, and diplomatic dimensions. Include a distinct section on AI's role (examples: targeting, surveillance, information operations, defense systems). Requirements and constraints:

1) Timeframe: prioritise reporting and official releases dated within the last 7 days (2026-03-08 to 2026-03-15 inclusive). If a crucial background citation is older, label it as background.
2) Sources: prioritize reputable outlets and primary sources (Reuters, AP, BBC, Al Jazeera, AFP, NYT, Washington Post, ICRC, UN OCHA, official ministry/DoD statements, credible think tanks like IISS/CSIS). Include direct URLs for each citation. Aim to fetch up to 10-12 pages (maxPages <= 12).
3) Structure: short headline; ~800 words total (target exactly 800 words if possible; acceptable ±5%). Sections: Opening summary (2–3 paragraphs); Military developments (recent operations, territorial changes, casualty figures with dates/sources); Political developments (leadership/coalition moves, domestic politics, sanctions

taskContract.requiredDeliverable: Produce a complete research-backed draft response.
doneCriteria: Gather source evidence from search/fetch outputs.
Return full draft text (not only status).

payload.inputJson: {"instruction":"Write a neutral, evidence-backed news article (~800 words, target 800 words ±5%) about developments in the Middle East war during 2026-03-08 through 2026-03-15. Structure: short headline; Opening summary (2-3 paragraphs); Sections titled: Military developments; Political developments; Humanitarian situation; Diplomatic moves; AI's role (targeting, surveillance, information operations, defense systems) as a distinct section; Closing note on outlook and sources. Requirements: - Prioritize and cite reputable outlets and primary sources (Reuters, AP, BBC, Al Jazeera, AFP, NYT, Washington Post, ICRC, UN OCHA, official ministry/DoD statements, IISS/CSIS). - Use only reporting or official releases dated within 2026-03-08 to 2026-03-15 for new/fresh claims; if a crucial background citation is older, label it as background. - Include direct URLs for each citation inline or as parenthetical footnote-like links at the end of the article. - Include casualty figures, dates, and sources where reported in the timeframe. - Tone: neutral, precise, and pragmatic. - Word limit: ~800 words (maxWords: 840). - Include at least 2 citations (preferably more). - If source material is weak or unavailable for a factual claim, explicitly say so and avoid making the claim. - Provide a source list (URLs) after the text. Use the fetched research material available and prioritize the latest official statements and reporting from 2026-03-08 to 2026-03-15. Output format: plain text article ready for copy/paste.","format":"news_article","maxWords":840}

input.instruction: Write a neutral, evidence-backed news article (~800 words, target 800 words ±5%) about developments in the Middle East war during 2026-03-08 through 2026-03-15. Structure: short headline; Opening summary (2-3 paragraphs); Sections titled: Military developments; Political developments; Humanitarian situation; Diplomatic moves; AI's role (targeting, surveillance, information operations, defense systems) as a distinct section; Closing note on outlook and sources. Requirements: - Prioritize and cite reputable outlets and primary sources (Reuters, AP, BBC, Al Jazeera, AFP, NYT, Washington Post, ICRC, UN OCHA, official ministry/DoD statements, IISS/CSIS). - Use only reporting or official releases dated within 2026-03-08 to 2026-03-15 for new/fresh claims; if a crucial background citation is older, label it as background. - Include direct URLs for each citation inline or as parenthetical footnote-like links at the end of the article. - Include casualty figures, dates, and sources where reported in the timeframe. - Tone: neutral, precise, and pragmatic. - Word limit: ~800 words (maxWords: 840). - Include at least 2 citations (preferably more). - If source material is weak or unavailable for a factual claim, explicitly say so and avoid making the claim. - Provide a source list (URLs) after the text. Use the fetched research material available and prioritize the latest official statements and reporting from 2026-03-08 to 2026-03-15. Output format: plain text article ready for copy/paste.

Write a neutral, evidence-backed news article (~800 words, target 800 words ±5%) about developments in the Middle East war during 2026-03-08 through 2026-03-15. Structure: short headline; Opening summary (2-3 paragraphs); Sections titled: Military developments; Political developments; Humanitarian situation; Diplomatic moves; AI's role (targeting, surveillance, information operations, defense systems) as a distinct section; Closing note on outlook and sources. Requirements: - Prioritize and cite reputable outlets and primary sources (Reuters, AP, BBC, Al Jazeera, AFP, NYT, Washington Post, ICRC, UN OCHA, official ministry/DoD statements, IISS/CSIS). - Use only reporting or official releases dated within 2026-03-08 to 2026-03-15 for new/fresh claims; if a crucial background citation is older, label it as background. - Include direct URLs for each citation inline or as parenthetical footnote-like links at the end of the article. - Include casualty figures, dates, and sources where reported in the timeframe. - Tone: neutral, precise, and pragmatic. - Word limit: ~800 words (maxWords: 840). - Include at least 2 citations (preferably more). - If source material is weak or unavailable for a factual claim, explicitly say so and avoid making the claim. - Provide a source list (URLs) after the text. Use the fetched research material available and prioritize the latest official statements and reporting from 2026-03-08 to 2026-03-15. Output format: plain text article ready for copy/paste.
